summary.intent: User wanted to wrap up a development session on OpenFang, then discovered the daemon wasn't running and wanted to authenticate using Claude Code OAuth token and Codex CLI token (not API keys) to enable both LLM providers with Codex as primary.
summary.outcome: Successfully integrated both CLI token authentication methods (Claude Code OAuth and Codex CLI token reading), updated config to use OpenAI/Codex as primary with Anthropic fallback, connected Telegram bot, removed Gemini agents, and fixed dashboard provider detection. However, discovered both tokens require CLI subprocess proxies rather than direct API access - ChatGPT OAuth tokens don't work against api.openai.com directly, and Claude Code OAuth tokens similarly need routing through their respective CLIs.

repo: OpenFang already had Claude Code OAuth token support via CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN in drivers/mod.rs:190-215
Codex CLI stores ChatGPT OAuth tokens in ~/.codex/auth.json under tokens.access_token with auth_mode field
Provider auth detection happens in ModelCatalog::detect_auth() which needs to check both env vars and CLI token files
The OpenAI driver constructor accepts base_url override for custom endpoints
Codex CLI uses https://chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex and /v1/responses API (not /v1/chat/completions)
Both codex exec and claude CLI support JSON output modes suitable for subprocess-based LLM drivers

code.finding: Added OpenAI special case to try Codex CLI token via read_codex_cli_token() before falling back to OPENAI_API_KEY env var, mirroring the Anthropic OAuth pattern
detect_auth() only checked env vars - needed to add has_codex_cli_token() check for openai and CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN check for anthropic to show providers as configured in dashboard
OpenAI driver makes standard /v1/chat/completions requests - incompatible with ChatGPT backend API format which uses /v1/responses endpoint

workflow: ChatGPT subscription OAuth tokens (from codex login) return 'insufficient_quota' against api.openai.com - they require ChatGPT-specific proxy endpoints
Claude Code OAuth tokens similarly don't work against standard api.anthropic.com endpoints
Both CLIs provide exec/proxy modes: 'codex exec' and 'claude -p' can be used as subprocess-based LLM drivers
Clippy warning: .or_else(|| function()) should be .or_else(function) without the closure wrapper
Agent spawning requires manifest_toml field with TOML config, not JSON fields in the POST body
Telegram bot requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN env var to be set when starting daemon

friction: ChatGPT OAuth tokens from Codex CLI don't work with standard OpenAI API - require reverse-engineering the proxy endpoint
Claude Code OAuth tokens also don't work with standard Anthropic API - require similar proxy discovery
No documentation on how to use ChatGPT subscription tokens programmatically outside the Codex CLI
Dashboard showed providers as 'missing' even when tokens were available because auth detection only checked env vars
Rate limiting errors from both providers made initial testing difficult
Cloudflare protection on chatgpt.com/backend-api prevented direct API testing
open_items: Implement subprocess-based LLM drivers that shell out to 'codex exec' and 'claude' CLI commands for proper OAuth token routing
Consider using OpenRouter API (key already available in env) as unified proxy for both GPT-4o and Claude models
Commit the 3 modified files: drivers/mod.rs, model_catalog.rs, and config.toml
Push checkpoint to Entire.io: git push fork entire/checkpoints/v1
Test end-to-end agent conversation flow once subprocess drivers are implemented
Investigate token exchange flow (RFC 8693) if direct API access becomes necessary for performance
